tree.setup: You're on the roof of your apartment building. Your roommate comes up to watch the sunset with you.
tree.setup_zh: 你在公寓楼的屋顶上。你的室友上来和你一起看日落。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 哇，好漂亮！你经常来这里吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Wā, hǎo piàoliang! Nǐ jīngcháng lái zhèlǐ ma?
turns.text_en: Wow, so beautiful! Do you come here often?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell them about your rooftop habit.

options.text_zh: 是的，我每天晚上都来看看。这里很安静。
options.text_pinyin: Shì de, wǒ měi tiān wǎnshang dōu lái kànkan. Zhèlǐ hěn ānjìng.
options.text_en: Yes, I come every evening to look. It's very quiet here.
options.feedback: Shares a quiet personal habit and explains why -- matches the peaceful rooftop moment.

options.text_zh: 有时候来。今天的日落很漂亮。
options.text_pinyin: Yǒu shíhou lái. Jīntiān de rìluò hěn piàoliang.
options.feedback: Answers and adds a nice observation, but doesn't share as much of yourself as the moment invites.

options.text_en: How did you get up here?
options.feedback: She's marveling at the sunset and you're asking about logistics -- breaks the beauty of the moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 真的好安静。我平时太忙了，都没有时间看天。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhēn de hǎo ānjìng. Wǒ píngshí tài máng le, dōu méiyǒu shíjiān kàn tiān.
turns.text_en: It really is quiet. I'm usually so busy, I never have time to look at the sky.

options.text_zh: 你以后可以常来。有时候停一停也好。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǐhòu kěyǐ cháng lái. Yǒu shíhou tíng yī tíng yě hǎo.
options.text_en: You can come often from now on. Sometimes it's good to stop for a moment.
options.feedback: Responds to her confession about being too busy -- a gentle invitation to slow down, shared from your own experience.

options.text_pinyin: Shì a, dàjiā dōu hěn máng.
options.feedback: Sympathizes but doesn't offer anything -- she opened up about missing the sky and you gave a generic response.

options.text_en: What time do you usually get off work?
options.feedback: She just said she's too busy to look at the sky, and you asked about her work schedule -- that's the opposite of slowing down.

tree.cultural_note: Rooftop moments in Chinese cities are rare and precious. In a culture that values hard work, the ability to simply stop and watch the sky is a quiet rebellion. These pauses are where healing begins.
